solution.p: To get rid of all fractions in this equation, we multiple all terms with the common denominator. In this equation, the common denominator is {x}.

p.m: \displaystyle{\begin{aligned} {\frac{3}{x}} \amp = {4+\frac{23}{x}} \\ \frac{3}{x} \cdot x \amp = 4 \cdot x +\frac{23}{x} \cdot x \\ 3 \amp = 4 x + 23 \\ 3 \mathbf{ - 23} \amp = 4 x + 23 \mathbf{ - 23} \\ -20 \amp = 4 x \\ \frac{-20}{4} \amp = \frac{4 x}{4} \\ -5 \amp = x \\ x \amp = -5 \end{aligned} }

solution.p: We must check solutions when we solve rational equations. By the original equation, the domain condition is x \ne 0. This doesnt conflict with the solution above, so the solution is x = -5.
